criteria.name: Architecture & design
criteria.description: Clear ingestion pipeline design, sensible component boundaries, README explains the data flow.

criteria.name: Implementation quality
criteria.description: Working code, readable structure, appropriate use of libraries and patterns for the module topic.

criteria.name: Data handling
criteria.description: Correct handling of datasets/events, validation, and storage or transformation logic.

criteria.name: Documentation & ops
criteria.description: Setup instructions, sample runs, and evidence the project can be evaluated without guesswork.

criteria.name: API design
criteria.description: REST/GraphQL/gRPC endpoints are coherent, documented, and match the brief.

criteria.name: Service implementation
criteria.description: Core service logic works; error handling and validation are present.

criteria.name: Deployment artifacts
criteria.description: Docker/K8s or equivalent deployment files exist and are plausible.

criteria.name: Testing & README
criteria.description: Evidence of manual or automated testing; clear run instructions.

criteria.name: Prompt & agent design
criteria.description: System prompts, tool use, or agent loops are purposeful and match requirements.

criteria.name: Integration
criteria.description: LLM provider integration is wired correctly with sensible guardrails.

criteria.name: Evaluation & examples
criteria.description: Sample inputs/outputs or eval notes demonstrate the agent behavior.

criteria.name: Code quality
criteria.description: Readable, modular code with a clear entry point.

criteria.name: MCP / integration surface
criteria.description: MCP server or enterprise adapter exposes the required tools/resources.

criteria.name: Security & scope
criteria.description: Reasonable boundaries, refusal behavior, and safe defaults.

criteria.name: Enterprise readiness
criteria.description: Deployment, config, or adoption notes suitable for a team rollout.

criteria.name: Write-up & repo hygiene
criteria.description: Write-up matches the repo; structure is navigable.
